config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya demi alasan “kepentingan সমাজ” karena itu mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan, terutama terhadap kelompok kecil atau yang tidak populer. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan tetap menghormati hak dasar setiap orang, bukan menempatkannya di bawah mayoritas begitu saja. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru dibangun dari perlindungan terhadap individu, bukan dari pengorbanan hak mereka.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come first over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse abuse by majorities and governments. Public safety and shared needs matter, but they should justify limits on individual rights only in narrow, well-defined cases, not as a default rule. If we flip the hierarchy and make “society” the top priority, it becomes too easy to trample unpopular people, which is exactly why individual rights exist. A healthy society is one that protects the common good without treating the person as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau menormalisasi pelanggaran hak yang seharusnya tidak bisa ditawar. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berpendapat, perlakuan adil, dan perlindungan dari kesewenang-wenangan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling patuh pada mayoritas, melainkan yang paling kuat menjaga hak setiap orang, terutama saat ada tekanan untuk mengorbankannya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that framing has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially against unpopular minorities. I do think there are moments when public safety or the common good can justify limited restrictions, but those limits need to be narrow, transparent, and accountable. Once you make “society” the higher principle, it becomes far too easy to excuse abuse in the name of the majority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan berlebihan terhadap kritik, minoritas, dan kebebasan berpendapat. Kepentingan bersama memang perlu, tetapi tidak boleh dijadikan alasan untuk menginjak hak dasar individu, karena justru di situlah negara dan mayoritas paling mudah menyalahgunakan kuasa. Menurut saya, yang benar adalah menyeimbangkan kepentingan publik dengan perlindungan hak, bukan menaruh masyarakat di atas individu secara otomatis. Kalau hak individu selalu bisa dikalahkan, maka yang disebut kepentingan সমাজ bisa berubah menjadi alat penindasan yang rapi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From a U.S. perspective, that kind of language has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially against unpopular people who have the least power to defend themselves. Public safety and the common good do matter, but they should only limit rights in narrow, accountable, and clearly justified cases, not because “society” is treated as a blank check. To me, a healthy society protects the common good by protecting individual rights first, not by making them easy to override.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
